theme: 5 contract red flags HR directors miss when booking incentive trips

linkedin.text:
There are 5 clauses in your incentive contract designed to be read against you. Most HR directors catch one.

After 200+ contracts in 16 years, here's where the budget quietly bleeds:

1️⃣ "Force majeure" with no refund schedule → vague means you pay 100% even if the destination shuts. Demand tiered refunds (60/90/120 days out).

2️⃣ F&B minimums with no carve-outs → that pizza night for 50 doesn't count toward your $40k. Exempt one off-property meal.

3️⃣ Single-room supplements that compound → +30% applied across every category means you pay it on suites too. Cap it to standard rooms.

4️⃣ Attrition with no release window → guarantee 100 rooms, 85 confirm, you eat the gap. Demand a 10-15% drop at 60-90 days.

5️⃣ A/V marked "as needed" → "flexible" becomes $850/day per mic on-site. Lock every price in writing.

The rule: any clause readable two ways will be read against you.

We negotiate all 5 in the client's favor on every contract — worth 4-7% of total budget on average.

💬 Comment "GUIDE" and we'll send our pre-signing checklist.

#IncentiveTravel #CorporateEvents #EmployeeEngagement #B2BEvents #MICE #Leadership

instagram.text:
5 contract traps that quietly eat your event budget 💼

After 200+ contracts in 16 years:

1️⃣ "Force majeure" with no refund schedule → vague = you pay 100%. Demand tiered refunds (60/90/120 days)
2️⃣ F&B minimums, no carve-outs → pizza nights don't count. Exempt 1 off-property meal
3️⃣ Compounding single-room supplements → suites pay +30% ON TOP. Cap to standard rooms
4️⃣ Attrition, no release window → 100 rooms, 85 confirm, you pay the gap. Demand 10-15% drop
5️⃣ A/V "as needed" → $850/day per mic on-site. Lock prices in writing

The rule: vague clauses get read AGAINST you. 🚨

Worth 4-7% of total budget when negotiated right.

facebook.text:
A client once almost signed a contract with a single line that would've cost them €31k. 💼

It was hidden in the "force majeure" clause — no refund schedule, so if the destination shut down, they paid 100%.

We see it constantly. The 5 traps that quietly drain budgets:
→ Force majeure with no refund schedule
→ F&B minimums with no carve-outs
→ Compounding single-room supplements
→ Attrition with no release window
→ A/V priced "as needed"

Any clause readable two ways gets read against you. Fixing them is worth 4-7% of the budget.

Got a contract on your desk right now? Send us a message before you sign. 💬
